_schema: comfy_spark LoRA catalog v1 for the LTX-2 19b base (separate from ltx2.loras.json, which is ltx2.3-22b). Maps a friendly name -> spec for a Tier-1 'drop-in' LoRA that only patches the MODEL (LoraLoaderModelOnly) and can be STACKED. comfy_launch.py reads this for --lora <name>[:<strength>] / --list-loras on a 19b preset (e.g. ltx19_generate). Camera-control LoRAs are the headline here: they steer the camera move. base MUST match the preset's base (ltx2-19b) or you get a loud warning.

_fields.file: filename to save as under models/loras (unique per stack)
_fields.base: compatible base model — must match the preset's model (ltx2-19b)
_fields.kind: effect | style | camera | motion | edit  (all drop-in/stackable)
_fields.trigger: phrase auto-appended unless --no-triggers (empty = none; camera LoRAs have NO trigger word)
_fields.prompt_example: ready-to-use starting prompt (do NOT include a trigger)

_CAMERA_NOTE: Per the Lightricks camera-control model cards there is NO trigger word. To get the best move, DESCRIBE THE DESTINATION of the movement in the prompt (where the camera ends up / what it reveals), not just 'dolly in'. They stack on the 19b base and can be combined (keep combined strength sane). Strength 1.0 is the loaded default; lower toward 0.7 if the move overpowers the scene.

dolly-in.note: Camera pushes IN toward the subject. Describe what the move reveals as it closes in.
dolly-in.prompt_example: the camera pushes in slowly past the foreground foliage to settle on the lone figure waiting at the end of the pier, golden hour, shallow depth of field

dolly-out.note: Camera pulls OUT/back, revealing the wider scene. Describe the wider environment it pulls back to show.
dolly-out.prompt_example: the camera pulls back from the candle flame to reveal the whole abandoned cathedral around it, dust in the air, soft volumetric light

dolly-left.note: Camera tracks LEFT (lateral truck). Describe what comes into frame on the left.
dolly-left.prompt_example: the camera tracks left along the market stalls, revealing a fire-eater performing for a gathering crowd, warm lantern light

dolly-right.note: Camera tracks RIGHT (lateral truck). Describe what comes into frame on the right.
dolly-right.prompt_example: the camera tracks right across the rooftop to reveal the neon skyline stretching into the rain, reflections on wet tiles

jib-up.note: Camera cranes UP (jib up), rising above the subject. Describe the high vantage it rises to.
jib-up.prompt_example: the camera cranes up from the street musician to reveal the full plaza and the mountains beyond, sweeping cinematic vista

jib-down.note: Camera cranes DOWN (jib down), descending toward the subject. Describe what it descends to meet.
jib-down.prompt_example: the camera cranes down from the treetops to settle on the deer drinking at the misty stream, dawn light filtering through leaves

static.note: Locks the camera STILL (no camera move) so only the subject moves — a true locked-off tripod shot. Useful when the base model adds unwanted drift.
static.prompt_example: a locked-off shot of a barista pulling an espresso shot, steam rising, the camera perfectly still, soft window light
